notes: PowerPoint-authored embedded-font oracle for docs/plans/embedded-fonts.md (Feature A import-carry merge test + Feature B author-side emit test). One blank slide whose text box 'silkscreen-text' uses both the regular and bold faces of the SIL OFL font 'Silkscreen', so PowerPoint embedded both faces under a single p:embeddedFont entry. PowerPoint COM SaveAs only ever SUBSETS embedded faces (the 'Embed all characters' radio is interactive-only; there is no COM property and the EmbedAllChars Options reg value is inert for COM-driven saves), so the .fntdata parts are subsetted glyph sets and saveSubsetFonts="1". This does not affect the structural oracle (embeddedFontLst / rels / content-type / part layout are identical regardless of subsetting), and a subsetted embed is what real-world decks most commonly carry. Feature B emits WHOLE faces; its byte source is the raw OFL .ttf under fonts/, not these subsetted parts.

rawFaces.note: Whole, un-subsetted OFL faces (the bytes Feature B embeds). fsType = Installable (embedding permitted). Family name 'Silkscreen' must match the embeddedFont typeface and any fontFace/run typeface for PowerPoint to bind it.
